extracts.extract: Data journalism involves investigative, in-depth, precision, computer-assisted and analytical journalism, to which three fruits of technological innovation are added: large volumes of data, interactive visualization, and incorporation of the programmer into the journalistic team.
extracts.relevance_rationale: The definition incorporating programmers on teams needs updating since LLMs now serve as programming intermediaries for journalists

extracts.extract: There are at least four different products that arise from data journalism: data-driven articles, interactive visualizations, open datasets, and news apps.
extracts.relevance_rationale: The four DJ product types -- articles, interactive visualizations, open datasets, news apps -- remain valid output categories

extracts.extract: In countries where there are no public information access laws or these don't work as they should, media outlets and organizations can devote themselves to the search, extraction and opening of public data. Since there are data that governments do not allow access to, media outlets can venture into that territory.
extracts.relevance_rationale: Media outlets role in extracting and opening data where FOI laws fail is a civic function that endures regardless of AI tools

extracts.extract: For data journalism one can do without many things, since there are free digital tools, but what we cannot do without is data. It is a necessary condition without which we cannot even begin to formulate any journalistic hypothesis.
extracts.relevance_rationale: Data as the sine qua non prerequisite for DJ -- you cannot begin without it -- remains true regardless of AI capabilities

extracts.extract: Visualizing data is not the same as accessing it. For data to be accessible it must be shown in a reusable format. An official document uploaded to Flickr as an image is not accessible — one would have to resort to OCR to extract the data, and these programs don't always work efficiently.
extracts.chapter: Visualizing data is not the same as accessing it
extracts.relevance_rationale: LLMs with vision capabilities can now extract data from images and PDFs far more reliably than the OCR tools described here

extracts.extract: When a CSV data file is downloaded, the columns must be restructured using Text to Columns in the Data tab, selecting Delimited with Tab and Comma separators.
extracts.chapter: Visualizing data is not the same as accessing it
extracts.relevance_rationale: Manual CSV restructuring with Text to Columns is now handled automatically by LLMs and modern data tools that auto-detect delimiters

extracts.extract: Short chronicles are suggested, with forceful conclusions and an important, dominant visualization in the article, when telling a story. Without an appropriate visualization accompanying the analysis of that data, it would be impossible to simply visualize the variables under study.
extracts.chapter: Short chronicles, with forceful conclusions and a strong visualization
extracts.relevance_rationale: Short narratives with forceful conclusions and dominant visualizations remain sound storytelling principles

extracts.extract: Comparison is perhaps one of the most used practices in journalism, but when a large amount of numerical information must be processed, the matter becomes complicated. Therefore, different ways of visualizing the dataset are used; in this way, we can better analyze certain processes and changes not detected at a glance.
extracts.chapter: Short chronicles, with forceful conclusions and a strong visualization
extracts.relevance_rationale: Using visualization to detect patterns not visible in raw numbers is an analytical principle that endures even with AI-assisted analysis

extracts.extract: Stating that data journalism requires a specialized visualization team as a sine qua non condition would establish an excessively large and dangerous gap between some and others. For those who have not received formal instruction, simpler ready-to-use resources remain.
extracts.chapter: For visualization beginners: ChartGo and, afterward, Many Eyes
extracts.relevance_rationale: LLMs and AI-powered chart tools have dramatically lowered the visualization skills gap, making specialized teams less of a prerequisite

extracts.extract: A GIS is software that allows a better visual representation of numerical data, as these are distributed geographically and spatially. Census data, for example, tend to be boring; although, managed through GIS programs, they can offer a clear view of the variables under study.
extracts.chapter: A GIS allows a better visual representation of numerical data
extracts.relevance_rationale: GIS for spatial analysis and geographic data visualization remains a valid technique; AI enhances but does not replace spatial reasoning

extracts.extract: Mashups allow the combination of data that exists in different sources, such as YouTube, Flickr, Google Maps and Google Earth. The scenario in which mashups are developed is incredibly versatile and new resources appear every day.
extracts.chapter: Mashups allow combining data from different sources
extracts.relevance_rationale: Mashup tools like Flickr/Google Maps integration described here have been superseded by modern APIs and LLM-powered data integration

extracts.extract: Google Earth is very useful when investigating companies or cases involving properties acquired by public officials, or the ecological impact of pollution. Google's advanced search allows finding places, people, companies by selecting the KMZ/KML format that returns Google Earth views.
extracts.chapter: Mashups allow combining data from different sources
extracts.relevance_rationale: Google Earth for investigating properties endures but satellite imagery AI and geospatial LLM tools have expanded these capabilities significantly

extracts.extract: Data journalism requires permanent training and knowledge of subjects generally far from the preferences of the conventional journalist. One must understand how the Public Administration works, know how to interpret laws, decrees and ordinances. If a journalist does not understand how the institutions of their country function, they will find it difficult to embark on the path successfully.
extracts.relevance_rationale: Understanding public administration, interpreting laws, and institutional knowledge are domain expertise requirements no LLM replaces

extracts.extract: Skills developed in web searching: using Google in the traditional way is not enough. One must learn to get more out of advanced searches, by format, by domain and by date; but also, in the absence of a national open data initiative, monitor the possible official sites where there is data.
extracts.relevance_rationale: Advanced Google search techniques by format, domain, and date have been largely supplanted by LLM-powered search and data discovery tools

extracts.extract: Have basic mathematics and statistics knowledge, both descriptive and inferential: a journalist who is innumerate will not have sufficient capacity to face the process known as the interview with the numbers. This is of particular interest when it comes to analysis of public budgets, taxes, crime and environmental contamination.
extracts.relevance_rationale: Basic numeracy endures as essential but LLMs can now scaffold the interview-with-numbers process for journalists with weaker math backgrounds

extracts.extract: The data journalist must always consider the possibility that, on occasion, there are no stories behind the data. Data journalism involves hard work — one must access dozens, if not hundreds or thousands of spreadsheets. Sometimes the routine is disappointing, because you won't always find what you're looking for.
extracts.relevance_rationale: Accepting that data may yield no story and that DJ requires persistence through disappointing results is a mindset principle that endures

extracts.extract: Often, the volume of data is so large that it is impossible to find a story unless an application is designed that allows us to group and analyze variables, for example, by geographic location, by date, by company name. News apps are the fruit of the joint work of journalists and computer programmers.
extracts.relevance_rationale: LLMs can now group and analyze large-volume variables without custom apps, reducing the dependency on programmer-journalist collaboration for this step
